name: Ducksfoot
definition: Leaflets fused (connate) into a single broad, webbed blade resembling a duck's webbed foot.
expression_rate: ~25% of F2 (3:1 ratio); ~50% from a true-breeding parent x carrier
origin: Popularised by Australian breeders; a deeper webbed-leaf lineage documented earlier in Western horticulture
first_documented: Modern line popularised late 1990s to early 2000s
notable_traits: Fused, webbed leaflets forming a single broad duck's-foot silhouette; low visual profile; structural only, does not impair vigour or cannabinoid production.
references: Grow Weed Easy; Sensi Seeds; community grower forums

name: Australian Bastard Cannabis
definition: Small, narrow, fleshy leaflets with smooth (entire) margins - serrations absent - giving a succulent-like look.
inheritance_stability: Stable when homozygous; recessive in outcrosses
expression_rate: ~25% of F2; F1 from an ABC x normal cross typically look normal
origin: Reportedly wild-found near Sydney, southern NSW / Victoria (community-reported)
first_documented: Reported 1970s-80s; named ABC and introduced worldwide in the 1990s
notable_traits: Small, narrow, fleshy, smooth-edged (toothless) leaves; succulent-like, non-cannabis appearance; hardiness/cold tolerance; cannabinoid production unaffected.
references: Medicinal Genomics; Sensi Seeds; community grower forums

name: Freakshow
definition: Thin, deeply divided and heavily serrated leaves resembling a fern frond rather than a cannabis fan leaf.
expression_rate: High in the stabilised line; segregates in outcrosses
origin: Bred by Shapeshifter (with Maxx) in California; commercialised by Humboldt Seed Company
first_documented: Selected/stabilised in the 2010s; to market via Humboldt Seed Company late 2010s
notable_traits: Thin, heavily serrated, deeply lobed fern-like leaves; sativa-leaning, ~18%+ THC, trace CBD, pine-lime aroma; seedlings mistaken for ferns/tomato sprouts.
references: Trichome Institute; Humboldt Seed Company; ICMag grower forums

name: Whorled Phyllotaxy
definition: Three or more leaves emerging from a single node in a ring instead of the usual opposite pair, often with an extra branch per node.
genetic_basis: Genetic (often somatic); mechanism not fully understood
expression_rate: Sporadic; often fades as the plant matures
first_documented: Long observed by growers; widely documented in cultivation literature
notable_traits: 3+ leaves and an extra branch per node; bushier plant with more budding sites; usually harmless; may slow growth slightly.
references: Mr Grow It; Royal Queen Seeds; Dutch Passion

name: Variegation
also_known_as: Variegated cannabis; partial albinism; white weed
definition: Partial loss of chlorophyll producing white or pale patches against normal green, sometimes a leaf split half-white/half-green.
inheritance_stability: Variable; patterning can pass through seed
expression_rate: Often needs a trigger such as hybridisation
origin: Appears spontaneously; can be triggered by hybridisation
first_documented: Long observed across many plant species and in cannabis
reproducibility: Partly heritable through seed; full albinos not viable
notable_traits: White/pale patches from lost chlorophyll; slower growth and lower yield; total albinos die young.
references: WeedSeedShop; Blimburn Seeds; Mr Grow It

name: Polyploid
definition: Three or more complete chromosome sets instead of the usual two (commonly tetraploid, four sets).
inheritance_stability: Unstable; often reverts to diploid or mixoploid
origin: Induced with colchicine or oryzalin; very rare in the wild
first_documented: Studied in cannabis research and hemp breeding programmes
notable_traits: Larger, thicker leaves; more vigour; higher flavonoid, protein and starch content; prone to reverting.
references: Frontiers in Plant Science; Dutch Passion; Kannabia

name: Creeper
definition: Heavy lower branches bow to the ground and grow horizontally, sometimes rooting at contact points; the related vine form grows thin, spiralling, hop-like stems.
genetic_basis: Genetic; tropical landraces (vine form linked to ABC)
expression_rate: Uncommon; favoured by large plants in humid tropical conditions
origin: Tropical strains; vine form arises from ABC crosses
first_documented: Described in grower literature; never commercialised
reproducibility: Not developed into any known strain
notable_traits: Ground-creeping branches that root at contact points, adding vigour and camouflage; related vine form has thin, spiralling, hop-like stems.
references: Sensi Seeds; Royal Queen Seeds; Zamnesia
